title: CR-091 VR Evidence Remediation — Interactive Authoring

comment:
PRE-REVIEW: CR-091-ADD-001 v0.1 — COMPLIANT. Recommend proceeding to pre-approval.

FRONTMATTER:
- title: "CR-091 VR Evidence Remediation — Interactive Authoring" — present, non-empty.
- revision_summary: "Initial draft" — acceptable for v0.1 of self-authorizing executable document per SOP-002 Section 8.

STRUCTURE: All 8 required sections present per TEMPLATE-ADD (Addendum Identification, Description of Omission, Impact Assessment, Correction Plan, Execution, Scope Handoff, Execution Summary, References).

PARENT STATE: CR-091 confirmed CLOSED at v2.0 — satisfies SOP-004 Section 9B.4.

CONTENT REVIEW:
- Section 2 clearly describes the omission: CR-091-VR-001 was authored as freehand markdown rather than through the interaction engine, producing assertional rather than observational evidence per SOP-004 Section 9C.5.
- Section 3 correctly scopes impact to the evidence artifact only; implementation and qualification are sound.
- Section 4 defines 4 EIs aligned with SOP-004 Section 5 (pre/post-execution baseline commits). EI-2 appropriately flagged with VR=Yes for behavioral verification.
- Section 5 EI table matches 6-column template format. Execution-time placeholders ([SUMMARY], [Pass/Fail]) are correct for pre-approval stage.
- Section 6 (Scope Handoff) follows SOP-004 Section 9B.5 three-item structure.
- Section 8 references all relevant governing documents including specific SOP-004 sections.

PLACEHOLDER CHECK: No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found. Square-bracket placeholders are execution-time editable fields per template convention.
FORMATTING: Consistent throughout. Section numbering sequential and correct.
TYPOS/ERRORS: None found.

POST-REVIEW: CR-091-ADD-001 v1.2 — NON-COMPLIANT. Two deficiencies identified in the attached VR (CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001 v1.1). The ADD execution content itself is well-structured, but the VR must be corrected before this ADD can proceed.

---

PART 1: CR-091-ADD-001 (ADD) REVIEW

FRONTMATTER:
- title: "CR-091 VR Evidence Remediation — Interactive Authoring" — present, non-empty.
- revision_summary: "Initial draft" — acceptable for self-authorizing executable document per SOP-002 Section 8.

STRUCTURE: All 8 required ADD sections present (Addendum Identification, Description of Omission, Impact Assessment, Correction Plan, Execution, Scope Handoff, Execution Summary, References).

PRE-APPROVED CONTENT (Sections 1-4): Unchanged from pre-review. Confirmed intact.

EXECUTION TABLE (Section 5):
- EI-1: Pass. Pre-execution baseline commit fa3f698 verified in git. Commit message: "CR-091-ADD-001 EI-1: Pre-execution baseline + VR closures."
- EI-2: Pass. VR column correctly updated from "Yes" to "CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001." Execution summary references 4 verification steps and 4 engine-managed commits (134c3d6, 0f2b01f, afb6a47, b86cfee) — all verified in git as [QMS] auto-commit entries.
- EI-3: Pass. VR checked in at v1.1 per audit trail. CR-091-VR-001 closure documented.
- EI-4: Pass. Post-execution state committed at b186690. Verified in git: "CR-091-ADD-001 EI-4: Post-execution state."
- All 4 EIs show Task Outcome: Pass. No Fail outcomes requiring VAR.
- All 4 EIs signed by claude with date 2026-02-22.

COMMIT CHAIN: fa3f698 -> 134c3d6 -> 0f2b01f -> afb6a47 -> b86cfee -> b186690. Clean linear chain, no unrelated commits interleaved.

EXECUTION COMMENTS: Two comments documented. First addresses orphaned VR closures (CR-090-VR-001, CR-092-VR-001). Second documents CR-091-VR-001 supersession per QA guidance — correctly references SOP-004 Section 9C.5, identifies the replacement VR, and confirms closure was sequenced after replacement VR was checked in.

SCOPE HANDOFF (Section 6): All three items complete. Parent accomplishments, ADD correction scope, and no-scope-loss confirmation all present and substantive.

EXECUTION SUMMARY (Section 7): Present and substantive. References all 4 engine-managed commits, VR compilation success, and CR-091-VR-001 closure.

CR-091-VR-001 STATUS: Confirmed CLOSED at v1.0 per audit trail (CLOSE event 2026-02-22T22:43:37). Supersession properly documented.

PLACEHOLDER CHECK: No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found in ADD.
FORMATTING: Consistent throughout.
TYPOS/ERRORS: None found in ADD.

ADD assessment: COMPLIANT.

---

PART 2: CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001 (VR) REVIEW per SOP-004 Section 9C.2

Per SOP-004 Section 9C.2, VRs are "reviewed as part of parent document's post-review." The following deficiencies were identified in the compiled VR at v1.1.

DEFICIENCY 1 — Empty title field in frontmatter.

Observed: title: '' (empty string)
Required: Per SOP-001 Section 5.1, the title field must be "present and non-empty."
Evidence: The CREATE audit event recorded the title as "Interaction System End-to-End Verification," but the compiled markdown frontmatter contains an empty string. The document heading also reads "# CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001: " with nothing after the colon.
Note: The VR itself acknowledges this in Step 2 outcome and the Summary narrative, characterizing it as "cosmetic." It is not cosmetic — it is a missing required frontmatter field.

DEFICIENCY 2 — Missing Signature section (Section 6).

Observed: The compiled VR ends at Section 5 (Summary) followed by "END OF VERIFICATION RECORD." There is no Section 6 (Signature) with Performer identity and date.
Required: Per SOP-004 Section 9C.4, VR content shall include "Signature: Performer identity and date." The TEMPLATE-VR defines performer and performed_date prompts after summary_narrative. These do not appear in the compiled output.
Impact: Without the Signature section, the VR lacks formal attribution of who performed the verification and when, which is a mandatory element per SOP-004 Section 9C.4.
Note: The Section 7 (References) defined in the template is also absent from the compiled output. This is less critical than the missing Signature but indicates the template was not fully traversed.

---

DISPOSITION: Request updates. Both deficiencies must be corrected before the ADD can proceed to post-approval.

RECOMMENDED CORRECTIVE ACTIONS:
1. Check out CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001, complete the remaining prompts (performer, performed_date) via qms interact, and check in again. This should produce a compiled VR with the Signature section.
2. Address the empty title field. If this is a qms-cli metadata propagation issue (title set at CREATE but not propagated to compiled frontmatter), document the root cause and ensure the compiled output contains the title.
3. After VR correction, update the ADD's execution summary if needed, check in the ADD, and re-route for post-review.

POST-REVIEW (re-review): CR-091-ADD-001 v1.2 — COMPLIANT. Recommend proceeding to post-approval.

This is a fresh post-review following disposition of two VR deficiencies identified in my prior review (2026-02-22T22:50:27). Finding 2 (missing Signature section) was withdrawn as erroneous — the VR correctly follows TEMPLATE-VR v5 which I approved under CR-098. Finding 1 (empty title) is formally dispositioned via CR-091-ADD-001-VAR-001 (Type 2, PRE_APPROVED v1.0).

---

SOP-002 SECTION 7.3 POST-REVIEW CHECKLIST:

1. EXECUTION COMPLETE: All 4 EIs show Task Outcome Pass. No Fail outcomes requiring VAR. VERIFIED.

2. BLOCKING VARs RESOLVED: CR-091-ADD-001-VAR-001 is Type 2, PRE_APPROVED at v1.0. Per SOP-004 Section 9A.2, Type 2 pre-approval is sufficient to unblock parent. VERIFIED.

3. DOCUMENTATION APPROVED: No SOPs or controlled documents modified by this ADD (other than VR child document). N/A.

4. EVIDENCE DOCUMENTED: All 4 EIs have substantive Execution Summary content with verifiable artifacts (commit hashes, document IDs). VERIFIED.

5. VRs COMPLETE: EI-2 flagged with VR column = CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001. VR checked in at v1.2. Reviewed below. VERIFIED.

---

- EI-2: Pass. VR column correctly updated from "Yes" to "CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001." Four engine-managed commits (134c3d6, 0f2b01f, afb6a47, b86cfee) verified in git as [QMS] auto-commit entries per SOP-004 Section 11.6.
- EI-3: Pass. VR checkin confirmed in audit trail (2026-02-22T22:43:29). CR-091-VR-001 closure documented.

EXECUTION COMMENTS: Three entries. (1) Orphaned VR closures (CR-090-VR-001, CR-092-VR-001). (2) CR-091-VR-001 supersession per QA guidance. (3) NEW: Full disposition of QA post-review findings — documents investigation of both deficiencies, QA withdrawal of Signature finding, and VAR creation/pre-approval. Complete and accurate audit trail.

PLACEHOLDER CHECK: No TBD, TODO, FIXME, or XXX found.

---

VR REVIEW (CR-091-ADD-001-VR-001 v1.2 per SOP-004 Section 9C.2):

STRUCTURE: 5 sections (Verification Identification, Verification Objective, Prerequisites, Verification Steps, Summary). This matches TEMPLATE-VR v5 (v3.0 EFFECTIVE) which does not include Signature or References sections.

CONTENT:
- Section 1: Parent CR-091-ADD-001, Related EI(s) "EI-2 (VR column)", Date 2026-02-22. Correct.
- Section 2: Verification objective clearly stated with per-response attribution.
- Section 3: Prerequisites specify repository, branch, commit (fa3f698), qms-cli submodule (d73f154), Python version, OS, template version. Sufficient for reproducibility per SOP-004 Section 9C.5.
- Section 4: Four verification steps exercising REQ-INT-009, -010, -012, -013, -015, -016, -017. Each step has Instructions, Expected, Actual (with atomic commit hash), and Outcome. All 4 Pass.
- Section 5: Overall Outcome Pass with substantive narrative.

EVIDENCE STANDARDS (SOP-004 Section 9C.5):
- Observational: Each step records actions and observations with quoted output. Not assertional. VERIFIED.
- Contemporaneous: Per-response timestamps. Engine-managed commit hashes on all step_actual responses. VERIFIED.
- Reproducible: Prerequisites and exact commands provided. VERIFIED.

PER-RESPONSE ATTRIBUTION: Every response has author (claude) and timestamp. Four commit-enabled responses have embedded commit hashes (134c3d6, 0f2b01f, afb6a47, b86cfee). This provides traceability equivalent to (and stronger than) a formal Signature section.

KNOWN DEFICIENCY — EMPTY TITLE FIELD: The VR frontmatter reads title: '' (empty string). This is formally dispositioned via CR-091-ADD-001-VAR-001 (Type 2, PRE_APPROVED). Root cause: CLI metadata propagation gap. The document is identifiable via QMS metadata (CREATE event title: "Interaction System End-to-End Verification"). Corrective action deferred to future CR.

VR assessment: COMPLIANT (with formally dispositioned deficiency).
